_meta.purpose: Phase-2 accuracy/parity eval set for the Icinga sub-agent (legacy vs Agent SDK).
_meta.provenance: Alert shapes are drawn from the REAL RHDP monitoring surface encoded in config/prompts/icinga_agent.md + skills/icinga-triage/SKILL.md (authored by the Parsec maintainers from production Icinga2). Case ocp-cluster-operators is the exact alert already used by parsec-dependencies/pr2-test. References are curated from the skill's domain logic and are PENDING SME validation by yvarbev@redhat.com (RHDP Icinga SME) and Eoghan's sign-off before they count as ground truth.
_meta.how_to_replace_with_real_history: Set source=mlflow (pull recent icinga runs from the parsec-agent-metrics experiment) or source=conversations (data/conversations/*.json on parsec-dev) once cluster/MLflow access is available; see scripts/parity_eval.py.

queries.query: Icinga alert: service 'OCP Cluster Operators' on host 'cnv-us-east-ocp-3' is CRITICAL (HARD). last_check_result.output: 'authentication operator Degraded=True (OAuthServerDeploymentDegraded); 1/33 operators degraded'. command: ['/home/icinga/monitoring-scripts/monitoring/check_ocp_cluster_operators.sh', '--degraded-crit', '1']. Not acknowledged, not in downtime. Triage it.

reference.platform: NaaS — nested OCP running as VMs on CNV (the cnv-* host name) on IBM Cloud bare metal
reference.root_cause: The authentication cluster operator is Degraded (OAuthServerDeploymentDegraded); 1 of 33 operators degraded crossed the check's --degraded-crit 1 threshold.
key_points: Identifies the degraded operator as authentication / OAuth server deployment
Names check_ocp_cluster_operators.sh and locates it in rhpds/monitoring-scripts
Notes cluster-operator config lives in the openshift group of rhpds/monitoring-config
Gives a 3-tier action plan (immediate / investigation / long-term)

queries.query: Dashboard alert: 'Babylon Schema YAML Diff on RHDP API Aggregator is critical'. Triage it.

reference.platform: RHDP API Aggregator / Babylon control plane
reference.root_cause: The deployed Babylon catalog schema YAML differs from the version in git (schema drift) — the diff check went CRITICAL.
key_points: Bridges the dashboard DISPLAY name to the internal check using match() with wildcards from keywords
Reports whether the alert is already acknowledged or in downtime first
Locates the check/config in the babylon-related group of rhpds/monitoring-config
Action plan addresses reconciling the schema diff

queries.query: Icinga: service 'OCP Nodes Ready' on host 'ocpv07-mgmt' is WARNING (HARD). Output: check_ocp_nodes_ready.sh reports 2/12 nodes NotReady. Triage it.

reference.root_cause: 2 of 12 nodes are NotReady, which crossed the WARNING (not yet CRITICAL) threshold in check_ocp_nodes_ready.sh.
key_points: Fetches check_ocp_nodes_ready.sh early because its threshold logic decides WARNING vs CRITICAL
Distinguishes WARNING from CRITICAL and notes the NotReady-node percentage drives severity
Gives node-level investigation commands (which nodes, why NotReady)

queries.query: Icinga: host 'cnv-us-west-ocp-2' is DOWN (HARD, state=1). Not acknowledged, not in downtime. Triage it.

reference.platform: NaaS — nested OCP on CNV (cnv-* host)
reference.root_cause: Host-level DOWN (reachability of the bare-metal/VM host), distinct from a single service going CRITICAL.
key_points: Treats host state 1=DOWN as a host-level (not service) problem
Considers parent-host / UNREACHABLE cascade
Checks whether it is in downtime before escalating
Uses the platform-appropriate bastion/access path

queries.query: Icinga: service 'Disk /var' on host 'rhpds-db-1' is CRITICAL (HARD). Output: 'DISK CRITICAL - free space: /var 2% (1.2 GiB)'. Triage it.

reference.root_cause: The /var filesystem is nearly full (2% free) — a resource/capacity alert.
key_points: Recognizes this is a RESOURCE alert where the service output is sufficient — does NOT need to search GitHub for config
Immediate mitigation: free space / rotate or clear logs
Long-term: capacity or log-retention fix

queries.query: Icinga: service 'Exam Scheduler Heartbeat' on host 'exams-prod-1' is CRITICAL (HARD), but it has an existing acknowledgement and an active scheduled downtime. Triage it.

reference.root_cause: Service is CRITICAL but already acknowledged AND in an active downtime window — most likely already being handled.
key_points: Reports the existing downtime/acknowledgement FIRST so engineers don't chase an already-handled issue
Pulls get_comments to surface notes from other engineers
Does NOT take write actions (ack/downtime/recheck) unless explicitly asked
